name: Basic Potato Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 2 cups All-Purpose Flour, Up to 2-1/2 Cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Puncture the potatoes in several places with a fork.
2. Bake for 1 hour at 350°F (175°C) or until tender. Remove and discard the skins.
3. Mash the cooled mashed potatoes into a large bowl, and let cool slightly.
4. Add the egg yolk, salt, and 2 cups flour to the mashed potatoes. Mix well.
5. Knead the dough on a work surface or in a bowl until it is soft and slightly sticky.
6. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour.
7. Lightly flour your hands and the work surface.
8. Break the dough into golf ball-sized pieces.
9. Roll each piece to a thickness of about a thumb.
10. Cut each gnocchi into 1-inch pieces.
11. Press each gnocchi against the back of a fork to create an imprint pattern. This helps them absorb more sauce and adds visual appeal.
12. Arrange the gnocchi on a floured tray.
13. Fill a large saucepan three-quarters full with salted water. Bring to a boil.
14. Add oil and dumplings to the boiling water. Stir gently to prevent sticking at the bottom of the pan.
15. When the dumplings float to the surface, let them cook for an additional 10-12 seconds.
16. Remove the dumplings with a slotted spoon when done. Be careful not to overcook, as they will absorb more moisture than regular pasta.
17. Serve with butter and Parmesan cheese or your favorite sauce.
